message:
fix(engine): interpreter implicit-return on call-stack underflow, Bayesian LLR asymmetry, and loss-selector cast ordering

baseline_interpreter: when execution falls off the end of the
instruction stream inside a function call, the interpreter now properly
pops the call frame, restores register_base, writes Undefined to the
return register, and continues at the caller's return_ip. Previously it
returned r0 from the callee's register window, which could be stale data
from a prior call. Also uses read_reg(0) instead of
self.registers.first() in the Halted handler to respect register_base.

bayesian_posterior: LLR step computation now handles asymmetric
likelihood ratios correctly. When malicious < benign, the ratio is
computed as -(ben-mal)/mal instead of (mal-ben)/ben, which would have
been negative overflow in unsigned arithmetic. Also handles zero
malicious likelihood (returns -MILLION) as a separate branch from zero
benign likelihood (+MILLION).

expected_loss_selector: abs_diff().div(10) now performs the division
before the as-i64 cast, preventing potential truncation where a u64
abs_diff exceeding i64::MAX would wrap to negative before division.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
